item.title: Amgen / Tepezza (Teprotumumab)
item.description:
• Summary: Amgen has reported positive clinical trial results for a subcutaneous, body-injected formulation of Tepezza (teprotumumab), its monoclonal antibody for Thyroid Eye Disease (TED). The new formulation demonstrated comparable efficacy and safety to the intravenous version, potentially offering greater patient convenience. This development is strategically important as Tepezza faces impending competition from biosimilars and other pipeline candidates. Amgen aims to bolster the product's lifecycle and market position with this more convenient administration option.
  • MoAs: Tepezza (teprotumumab): Monoclonal antibody that binds to and inhibits the insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor (IGF-1R), reducing inflammation and tissue remodeling in TED.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, Endpoints News, Fierce Pharma
